translations.en: Know you all, O slaves of Allah! A believer acts to earn the reward for three purposes: either it is for the good of this world, then Allah rewards him for his deed in his lifetime. As Allah said for Ibrahim: 'And We gave him his reward in this life, and in the hereafter he shall be among the righteous' [29:27]. So whoever works for Allah, He gives him his reward in this world and the hereafter, and becomes sufficient for him in both abodes. Indeed, Allah says: 'O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. Those who have been bounteous in this world will have a bounty, and Allah's earth is vast. The steadfast will be paid their wages in full without reckoning' [39:10]. And He will not take account for what He gives them here. He says: 'To those who do the good deed is the best reward and more' [10:26]. So the best reward is Paradise, and more is what he is rewarded here. Or he works purely for the next world – for him Allah expiates every sin with good deed. He says: 'Surely, the good deeds will wipe out the evil deeds, and that is a reminder for those who remember' [11:114]. Till on the Day of Judgement, his good deeds will be counted, and Allah will reward him ten fold to seven hundred fold for every single act. Allah says: 'A reward from your Lord, a sufficient gift' [78:36], 'To those who will be meted out the double reward, and they shall be secure in the lofty chambers' [34:37]. So be desirous of this, may Allah have mercy on you, and work hard for it, and urge each other to do accordingly.
